response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's important to be aware of the potential for misinformation and take steps to verify the accuracy of what you see. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or too-good-to-be-true posts, as they may be clickbait or propaganda. 2) Check the source of the information, especially if it's from an unfamiliar source. 3) Look for corroboration from other sources to confirm the accuracy of the information. 4) Use fact-checking tools and websites to verify the truth of a claim. 5) Consider the motivations behind the post and whether it's possible that the creator has an agenda. By taking these steps, you can better navigate social media and avoid falling for fake content."
,"reasoning_summary": "This answer is based on the following reasoning: 1) Being cautious of sensational or too-good-to-be-true posts can help identify clickbait or propaganda. 2) Checking the source of information can help confirm its accuracy. 3) Corroboration from other sources can provide additional evidence. 4) Using fact-checking tools and websites can verify the truth of a claim. 5) Considering the motivations behind a post can help identify potential agendas."
,"tone": "informative"

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's important to be aware of the potential for misinformation and take steps to verify the accuracy of what you see. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of sensational or too-good-to-be-true posts, as they may be clickbait or propaganda. 2) Check the source of the information, especially if it's from an unfamiliar source. 3) Look for corroboration from other sources to confirm the accuracy of the information. 4) Use fact-checking tools and websites to verify the truth of a claim. 5) Consider the motivations behind the post and whether it's possible that the creator has an agenda. By taking these steps, you can better navigate social media and avoid falling for fake content.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: This answer is based on the following reasoning: 1) Being cautious of sensational or too-good-to-be-true posts can help identify clickbait or propaganda. 2) Checking the source of information can help confirm its accuracy. 3) Corroboration from other sources can provide additional evidence. 4) Using fact-checking tools and websites can verify the truth of a claim. 5) Considering the motivations behind a post can help identify potential agendas.
